name: NDC Transport Tracker Taxonomy
citation: GIZ and SLOCAT (2025). NDC Transport Tracker (vers. 4.0). Available from: www.changing-transport.org/tracker.
definition_policy: Definitions are preserved verbatim in 'definition'. Where a clarified version is offered it appears in 'definition_suggested' (null when no change is suggested). The maintainer decides which to adopt. Source typos are preserved in 'source_original' when corrected.

Target area.label: Net zero target
Target area.definition: This parameter captures any economy-wide net zero targets.

Target area.label: Overall mitigation target
Target area.definition: A reduction target for greenhouse gas emissions (covering CO2 and other relevant greenhouse gases) has been set for the whole economy or collectively for all sectors covered in the document.

Target area.label: Transport sector mitigation target
Target area.definition: A greenhouse gas emission mitigation target has been set for transport or a transport subsector on the national level.

Target area.label: Transport sector adaptation target
Target area.definition: A quantifiable target has been set for transport adaptation and resilience on the national level.

Target area.label: Energy sector target
Target area.definition: A mitigation target for the energy sector (and thus indirectly for transport) has been adopted on the national level. Any transport-related figure presented within the energy target is counted exclusively as a transport target.

Conditionality.label: Unconditional
Conditionality.definition: This target is not conditional or contingent upon any other developments.

Conditionality.label: Conditional
Conditionality.definition: This target is conditional or contingent upon international/external support.

Conditionality.label: Unclear conditionality
Conditionality.definition: The document is not clear whether the target is unconditional or conditional.

GHG target?.label: GHG
GHG target?.definition: The target specifies a value in terms of GHG that will be reduced or avoided

GHG target?.label: Non-GHG
GHG target?.definition: The target DOES NOT specifiy a value in terms of GHG that will be reduced or avoided

Target type.label: GHG: Base year
Target type.definition: A commitment to reduce, or control the increase of, emissions by a specified quantity relative to a historical base year. For example, a 25% reduction from 1990 levels by 2020. These are sometimes referred to as “absolute” targets.

Target type.label: GHG: BAU
Target type.definition: A commitment to reduce emissions by a specified quantity relative to a projected emissions baseline scenario

Target type.label: GHG: Fixed level
Target type.definition: A commitment to reduce, or control the increase of, emissions to a specified emissions quantity in a target year/period. Fixed-level target include carbon neutrality targets or phase-out targets, which aim to reach zero net emissions by a specified date. For example, zero net emissions by 2050.

Target type.label: GHG: Base year intensity
Target type.definition: A commitment to reduce emissions intensity (emissions per unit of another variable, typically GDP) by a specified quantity relative to a historical base year. For example, a 40% reduction below 1990 base year intensity by 2020.

Target type.label: GHG: Trajectory
Target type.definition: A commitment to reduce, or control the increase of, emissions to specified emissions quantities in multiple target years or periods over a long time period (such as targets for 2020, 2030, and 2040 over the period 2020- 2050). Trajectory targets also include “peak-and decline” targets.

Target type.label: Other GHG targets
Target type.definition: GHG targets that do not fit in any other indicator

Target type.label: Renewable energy
Target type.definition: A mitigation target for renewable energy such as capacity or production targets

Target type.label: Avoid targets
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets only: targets related to avoided travel

Target type.label: Mode share targets
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets only: targets related to changes in mode share

Target type.label: Infrastructure targets
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets only: targets related to infrastructure investment or specific amounts of infrastructure constructed

Target type.label: Vehicle efficiency targets
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets only: targets related to changes in vehicle efficiency

Target type.label: Zero emission vehicle targets
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets only: targets related to electrification of vehicles and introduction of zero emission vehicles

Target type.label: Biofuel targets
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets only: targets related to biofuel

Target type.label: Renewable energy in transport targets
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets only: targets related to renewable energy use in transport in general

Target type.label: Non-national transport GHG mitigation target
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets only: targets focusing on transport GHG mitigation reduction on a subnational level (NEW)

Target type.label: Other non-GHG transport targets
Target type.definition: Non-GHG transport targets that do not fit in any other indicator

Target type.label: General adaptation targets
Target type.definition: All adaptation targets (could be further refined)

Target scope.label: Overall mitigation target
Target scope.definition: Targets that cover the whole economy

Target scope.label: Sector-wide
Target scope.definition: Targets that cover the total sector for transport, energy, renewables and adaptation targets

Target scope.label: Subsector targets
Target scope.definition: Targets that cover one sector more specified sub-subsectors of the transport, energy or renewables sector or for adaptation

Target scope.label: Unclear scope
Target scope.definition: Targets where the scope is not clear

ICE phase-out target type.label: Sales all vehicles
ICE phase-out target type.definition: Targets that declare a general ban to sell of new ICE vehicles in a market  by a specific point of time.

ICE phase-out target type.label: Sales cars
ICE phase-out target type.definition: Targets that declare a ban to sell new ICE cars in the market by a specific point of time.

ICE phase-out target type.label: Sales public transport
ICE phase-out target type.definition: Targets that declare a ban to sell new ICE public transport vehicles in the market  by a specific point of time.

ICE phase-out target type.label: Sales unclear scope
ICE phase-out target type.definition: Targets that declare a ban to sell new ICE vehicles in the market  by a specific point of time. The scope of vehicles targeted is not clear.

ICE phase-out target type.label: Fleet all vehicles
ICE phase-out target type.definition: Targets that declare a general ban to use of ICE vehicles as part of a fleet  by a specific point of time.

ICE phase-out target type.label: Fleet cars
ICE phase-out target type.definition: Targets that declare a ban to use ICE cars as part of a fleet  by a specific point of time.

ICE phase-out target type.label: Fleet public transport
ICE phase-out target type.definition: Targets that declare a  ban to use ICE public transport vehicles as part of a fleet  by a specific point of time.

ICE phase-out target type.label: Fleet unclear scope
ICE phase-out target type.definition: Targets that declare a  ban to use ICE  vehicles as part of a fleet  by a specific point of time. The scope of vehicles targeted is not clear.

Type of measure: category.label: Mode shift and demand management
Type of measure: category.definition: This category is for measures that encourage more environmentally friendly transport modes or that reduce overall demand for motorised transport. It includes several pricing-related measures (taxes, subsidies and other economic instruments).

Type of measure: category.label: Transport system improvements
Type of measure: category.definition: This category is for mitigation measures that address the entire transport system and closely related sectors. It covers planning, land use and infrastructure measures, but also considers transport system improvements, intelligent transport systems,  freight demand management and transport-related education activities.

Type of measure: category.label: Energy efficiency
Type of measure: category.definition: This category is used for measures that seek to improve the energy efficiency of vehicles and the transport system as a whole.

Type of measure: category.label: Electrification
Type of measure: category.definition: This category includes mitigation measures that focus on the electrification of transport, including electric vehicle uptake, purchase incentives and charging

Type of measure: category.label: Alternative fuels
Type of measure: category.definition: This category considers measures related to the use of alternative fuels (e.g. ethanol, hydrogen, LPG) except for electric mobility

Type of measure: category.label: Aviation and maritime
Type of measure: category.definition: This category includes innovations in aviation, shipping. They are treated as a category apart because they represent areas, for which few mitigation options exist.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Transport demand management
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose covers various strategies that address transport demand. Such measures aim to act on the transport demand (km traveled) and the choice of modes by encouraging low-carbon modes (such as walking, cycling or public transport) and discouraging the use of high-emission modes.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Economic conditions for sustainable transport
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose refers to the overarching economic structures and conditions in which people make transport-related choices (such as buying a vehicle, chosing a mode to travel). It covers green procurement, carbon-related pricing and finance mechanisms as well as taxes and subsidies for transport decarbonization.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Digital solutions
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose groups measures that enhance the efficiency of modes/transport systems through digital solutions

Type of measure: purpose.label: Promote active mobility
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose groups measures that contribute to promoting active mobiliy (also called non-motorised transport), such as cycling and warlking, wheelchair movement

Type of measure: purpose.label: Public transport improvement
Type of measure: purpose.definition: The purporse  covers all measures designed to improve or further develop the public transport system.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Enhance comprehensive transport planning
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose considers planning tools and overall planning frameworks to support sustainable transport.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Improve infrastructure
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose covers measures that focus on improving and expanding infrastructure and its utilization and ensure intermodality by interlinking different modes of transport.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Freight efficiency improvements
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose covers measures for the improvement of the freight transport system.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Education and behavioral change
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose covers measures targeting a change of behaviour through education and awareness

Type of measure: purpose.label: Promote vehicle improvements
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose refers to  improvements that enhance the energy efficiency of  vehicles.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Promote electric mobility
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose covers measures that seek to foster the transition to electric mobility.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Promote alternative fuels
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose considers measures aimed at promoting the use of alternative fuels.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Promote improvements in aviation
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose considers measures aimed at promoting technical improvement in the aviation sector.

Type of measure: purpose.label: Promote improvements in shipping
Type of measure: purpose.definition: This purpose considers measures aimed at promoting technical improvement in the shipping sector.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Transport demand management measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter records any general mention of activities focusing on reducing demand for motorized transport.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Car access restriction and low emission zones
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to car access measures that restrict the physical access of certain types of vehicles to certain places (e.g. city centres). An example are Low Emission Zones. By resticting access based on certain criteria (type of propulsion technique, euro-Standards, age etc) access limitations can have differentiated impact such as GHG emissions reductions, air pollutants reduction and congestion prevention. They can encourage the use of low-carbon modes like cycling and public transport and/or support technological shift to cleaner technologies.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: This parameter refers to measures that restrict the physical access of certain vehicle types to certain places (e.g. city centres); Low Emission Zones are one example. By restricting access based on criteria such as propulsion technology, Euro standard or age, these measures can deliver differentiated impacts (GHG reduction, lower air pollution, congestion prevention) and can encourage low-carbon modes and a shift to cleaner technologies.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Commuter trip reduction policies
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to the management of circumstances and incentives for employee commuter travel and working arrangements to reduce traffic and automobile use.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Parking measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to actions that aim to improve parking management such as pricing, quantity restrictions, parking reform etc. which then also contribute to a reduced demand of motorised travel.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General economic instruments
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter records any general mention of economic instruments, such as taxes& tax breaks, fees, duties and subsidies, which contribute to integrating  environmental costs and benefits into the budgets of households and firms.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Emissions trading and carbon pricing
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to emissions trading systems (ETS) or cap-and-trade systems:  a pricing mechanism for emitted greenhouse gas emissions. Unlike a direct carbon tax, where the unit price of CO2 is fixed, under an emissions trading scheme, the price per tonne of CO2 varies. The overall amount of emissions is fixed for a given period of time (e.g. annually). Entities are allocated a set amount of CO2 emissions allowances, or quotas, and trade emissions with each another. Those able to reduce their emissions below their allowance level can trade them with those emitting in excess of their allowance.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Financial instruments to support decarbonisation
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter records financing instruments used to pay for technologies, projects and programmes that reduce GHG emissions. Financing instruments that aim to support decarbonization include climate finance solutions, investments in EVs, green bonds, etc. Not to confound with economic instruments!
Type of measure: instruments.source: ITF (2020): Financial instruments to support decarbonisation
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: This parameter records financing instruments used to pay for technologies, projects and programmes that reduce GHG emissions, e.g. climate finance solutions, investments in EVs, green bonds. Not to be confused with economic instruments.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Fossil fuel subsidy elimination
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to policies and decisions that eliminate or reduce subsidies for fossil fuels. Energy subsidies are used by governments to lower the cost of producing or consuming fossil fuels. Eliminating such subsidies can help to reduce reliance on fossil fuels.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Fuel taxation
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter records national or local taxes on the sale of fuel. Every fuel type is taxed differently. One target of taxing fuel is to reduce fuel consumption and encourage more efficient transport modes.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Green public procurement
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to stakeholders to taking into account environmental impacts when procuring goods and services. Applied to transport, it means that a public authority can develop green procurement regulations that, for example, only allow the purchase of zero-emission vehicles. Such measures can support the transition to cleaner public vehicle fleets and more sustainable consumption.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Road charging and tolls
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to surcharges applied to general or specific road use, including in particular highway tolls. This includes congestion pricing.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Vehicle taxation
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to  taxes on vehicle pruchase and/or ownership
Type of measure: instruments.source: ITF (2020): Vehicle purchase and ownership taxes

Type of measure: instruments.label: Measures supporting autonomous vehicles
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter identifies measures that promote self-driving vehicles, artificial intelligence and any other mechanisms that support the automation of passenger and freight transport.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Data and modelling improvements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter identifies any measures related to transport data (e.g. collection, analysis or application) as well as models designed to predict traffic flows or transport demand growth.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General innovations and digitalization
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter includes activities that mention the use of innovation and digitalization to improve the efficiency of transport.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Intelligent transport systems
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to intelligent transport systems that  harness technology to improve the management and operation of transport services. Relevant technologies include sensors, wireless communications, notification systems and other ICT solutions.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Shared mobility
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter includes general measures in the area of shared mobility, such as bike sharing, car-sharing, shared scooters etc..

Type of measure: instruments.label: General active mobility
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter is used for general measures that refer to walking and cycling are included here.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: General measures that refer to walking and cycling are included here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Cycling measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter covers any action that specifically mentions improving cycling is included here.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: Any action that specifically mentions improving cycling is included here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Walking measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter covers any action that specifically mentions improving walking is included here.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: Any action that specifically mentions improving walking is included here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: BRT
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to Bus rapid transit (BRT): a bus system with high speed, capacity, punctuality and operating flexibility. Common characteristics of a BRT system include the use of bus-only lanes, advance ticketing, and articulated buses.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Public transport integration and expansion
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter covers activities that aim to expand public transport or integrate different public transport services into a single system.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Express lanes/ public transport priority
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter looks at actions that give priority to public transport over other modes. Examples include transit signal priorities, access priority, intelligent transport systems and express lanes.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General public transport improvement
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter covers all activities that aim to improve the public transport system.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General transport and land use planning
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter records any general mention of transport planning.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Traffic management improvements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter looks at management, infrarstructure and techonlogical approaches with the goal of improving traffic flow.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: This parameter looks at management, infrastructure and technological approaches with the goal of improving traffic flow.

Type of measure: instruments.label: National mobility plans
Type of measure: instruments.definition: A national mobility plan provides a comprehensive long-term planning framework for the transport sector. It features a vision and timeframes for action at the national level. This parameter records these planning activities that focus on nationwide transport.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Sustainable urban mobility plans
Type of measure: instruments.definition: A sustainable urban mobility plan (SUMP) is “a strategic plan designed to satisfy the mobility needs of people and businesses in cities and their surroundings for a better quality of life. It builds on existing planning practices and takes due consideration of integration, participation and evaluation principles”. If the document refers to a SUMP or an integrated approach on urban mobility, this is captured here.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: A sustainable urban mobility plan (SUMP) is a strategic plan designed to satisfy the mobility needs of people and businesses in cities and their surroundings for a better quality of life. It builds on existing planning practices and gives due consideration to integration, participation and evaluation principles. Captured here when a document refers to a SUMP or an integrated urban mobility approach.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Access improvement instruments (density, mixed use)
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter captures different features of the urban built environment that contribute to low mileage and sustainble travel patterns. This inclues urban density, land-use diversity "mixed used" and enhanced accessibility.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Expansion of infrastructure
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to activities that aim to introduce new infrastructure or expand infrastructure for transport . If a measure is dedicated to a specific transport mode, then it might be captured under that specific parameter. Any general mention of expanding transport infrastructure is collected here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Infrastructure improvements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter is for measures that outline general improvements in transport infrastructure or the transport system as a whole, without providing details about specific measures.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Intermodality measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Intermodality is the combination of different transport modes with the goal of enabling convenient, seamless transfer between them. Any general activities that highlight intermodality but do not specify actions are included here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General freight efficiency improvements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter records general efficiency improvements in freight. If the document does not specify a specific activity or action that belongs to the other freight efficiency activities, then it is captured here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Improving load
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter is for measures that encourage reliance on high-capacity vehicles (trains, ships, etc.) in order to achieve lower carbon intensity per ton transported.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Freight transport shifting to rail or inland waterways
Type of measure: instruments.definition: In freight transport, intermodality often entails a shift to less carbon-intensive transport modes (e.g. rail and waterborne transport). This parameter records any actions that support a shift of road freight to rail or waterways.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Ecodriving
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Ecodriving refers to educational measures that encourage more efficient driving practices. Such practices can reduce fuel consumption and are captured in this parameter.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: Ecodriving refers to educational measures that encourage more efficient driving practices, which can reduce fuel consumption. Captured under this parameter.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General education and behavior change
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter is for general educational activities and behavioral change related to transport, e.g. concerning the environmental impacts of private vehicle use, the benefits of electric vehicles, etc.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: This parameter is for general educational activities and behavioural change related to transport (e.g. the environmental impacts of private vehicle use, the benefits of electric vehicles, etc.).

Type of measure: instruments.label: Labelling requirements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to measures requiring publication of information on environment impacts, this environmental impact can be GHG emissions, fuel consumption, carbon intesntiy of a fuel or local pollutants. All of the different labels are captured under this parameter.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Vehicle air pollution emission standards
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter captures emission standards that regulate air pollution exhaust emission(such as NOx) such as the EURO standards Euro1-6 (not referring to CO2 standards)
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: This parameter captures emission standards regulating air-pollutant exhaust emissions (such as NOx), e.g. the EURO standards Euro 1-6 (not referring to CO2 standards).

Type of measure: instruments.label: Fuel quality improvements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: A high-quality fuel contains very low levels of sulfur. Countries set fuel quality standards in order to guarantee fuel quality. This parameter covers any mention of clean fuels or better fuel quality in the transport sector

Type of measure: instruments.label: Inspection and maintenance
Type of measure: instruments.definition: A well-maintained vehicle can ensure higher energy efficiency. This parameter considers measures that pertain to vehicle inspections or maintenance.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Vehicle efficiency standards
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter captures measures designed to improve vehicle efficiency or lower transport emissions. This is done through fuel economy  (or CO2) standards, which regulate how far a vehicle must travel when consuming a given quantity of fuel (e.g. in liters per 100 km or miles per gallon or CO2/100km).

Type of measure: instruments.label: General vehicle improvement measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter identifies any general vehicle improvement measures that are included in the document.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Vehicle restrictions (import, age, access, sale, taxation)
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter encompasses various restrictions to vehicle ownership or purchase, including import bans on older vehicles or sale restrictions on particularly polluting vehicles.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: This parameter encompasses restrictions on vehicle ownership or purchase, including import bans on older vehicles or sale restrictions on particularly polluting vehicles.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Vehicle scrappage scheme
Type of measure: instruments.definition: In order to support the transition to cleaner, more efficient vehicles, governments may provide incentives when an owner scraps their current, old vehicle (rather than reselling it), circular economy.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General e-mobility measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Any general policies that refer to electric mobility without specifying a transport mode or specific measure are covered by this parameter.

Type of measure: instruments.label: EV charging infrastructure measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Electric vehicle charging infrastructure is needed to promote the adoption of electric vehicles. Measures that seek to increase the number of public charging stations or facilitate more private/public charging points are covered here

Type of measure: instruments.label: EV purchase incentives
Type of measure: instruments.definition: National and local governments can support the transition to e-mobility by providing financial incentives for the purchase of electric vehicles.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Smart charging policies
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Smart charging refers to systems that optimize electric vehicle charging by prioritizing off-peak hours or times of high variable renewable feed-in.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General micromobility
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Micromobility refers to electric personal transportation devices, such as electric kick-scooters and other electric-powered devices, not covered under shared mobility.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General alternative fuels measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Any general reference to the use of alternative fuels in the transport sector is recorded here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Low emission vehicle purchase incentives
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to purchase incentives granted to consumers for lower emission vehicles (excluding electric and hybrid vehicles).

Type of measure: instruments.label: Biofuels support measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Conventional diesel and gasoline can be mixed with less carbon-intense fuels. Many national governments set blending mandates (for example, 10% or 20% of diesel has to be biofuel). The most common biofule is ethanol. Any general biofuel blending mandates as well as specific methons of ethanol (or other biofuls) are covered here.
Type of measure: instruments.definition_suggested: Conventional diesel and gasoline can be blended with less carbon-intensive fuels. Many national governments set blending mandates (e.g. 10% or 20% biofuel). The most common biofuel is ethanol. General biofuel blending mandates as well as specific mentions of ethanol (or other biofuels) are covered here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Hydrogen support measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to this  relatively new fuel in the transport sector; hydrogen is used in fuel-cell electric vehicles. Green hydrogen that is produced using renewable electricity is seen as one important component of the energy transition in transport.

Type of measure: instruments.label: LPG/CNG/LNG support measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter is for measures that refer to liquified petroleum gas (LPG), compressed natural gas (CNG) or liquified natural gas (LNG) in the transport sector.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General renewable energy support measures
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Renewable energy for transport looks at the use of biofuels, green hydrogen and green electricity. This parameter captures any actions that make a direct link between transport and renewables.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Aircraft fleet renovation
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Newer aircraft are generally more energy efficient. This parameter refers to activities designed to renew the aircraft fleet or only allow newer aircraft to operate.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Air traffic management
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Any measures that focus on improving air traffic are referred to here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General aviation improvements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: Any general measures that focus on the aviation sector are referred to here.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Airport CO2 certification
Type of measure: instruments.definition: CO2 certification systems aim to mitigate greenhouse gas emissions by airports and ground operations. This parameter is for initiatives designed to improve the energy efficiency and carbon footprint of airports.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Jet fuel policies
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to policies designed to lower the carbon intensity of fuels for aviation or to introduce alternative fuel sources, including biofuel blending mandates.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Support on-shore power and electric charging facilities in ports
Type of measure: instruments.definition: While low-carbon fuels for ships are still being explored, there are already several solutions for providing electricity to vessel when docked. This is also commonly known as “cold ironing”.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Port infrastructure improvements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to improvements to ports and other shore-based facilities.

Type of measure: instruments.label: Ship efficiency improvements
Type of measure: instruments.definition: The parameter identifies actions that aim to improve the energy efficiency of ships.

Type of measure: instruments.label: General shipping improvement
Type of measure: instruments.definition: This parameter refers to any general measure that targets shipping, maritime transport or inland navigation.

Type of measure: instruments.label: No concrete instrument
Type of measure: instruments.definition: NEW! Can apply to all categories & purposes

Category.label: Structural and Technical
Category.definition: The category addresses structural solutions (infrastructure development and refurbishment) and technical solutions (risk modelling and other technologies) designed to address climate change risks or impacts.

Category.label: Informational and Educational
Category.definition: Adaptation measures related to monitoring and warning systems, disaster planning, and education and training.

Category.label: Institutional and Regulatory
Category.definition: This category covers measures in the fiels of regulations (laws, norms, policies) and institutions (creation of an authority, extension of responsbilities etc.)

Category.label: Other adaptation and resilience measures
Category.definition: This category groups measures that do not fit in the previous categories

Measure.label: Transport Infrastructure Resilience
Measure.definition: This parameter identifies efforts to adapt transport infrastructure to climate change impacts and to increase its resilience
Measure.definition_suggested: This parameter identifies efforts to adapt transport infrastructure to climate-change impacts and to increase its resilience.

Measure.label: Transport System Adaptation
Measure.definition: This parameter identifies efforts to adapt to climate change impacts to transport infrastructure and to increase ist resilience
Measure.definition_suggested: This parameter identifies efforts to adapt the transport system to climate-change impacts and to increase its resilience.

Measure.label: Risk assessment
Measure.definition: This parameter identifies efforts to understand risks and impacts to the transport system (e.g. through modelling).

Measure.label: Resilient transport technologies
Measure.definition: This parameter identifies efforts to adopt resilient transport technologies (e.g. climate resilient materials for streets or cars).

Measure.label: Repair and maintenance
Measure.definition: This parameter identifies any efforts to maintain and repair infrastructure and transport systems.

Measure.label: Monitoring
Measure.definition: This parameter identifies efforts to adopt  monitoring systems, e.g. to detect risks early on.

Measure.label: Education and Training
Measure.definition: This parameter records efforts to educate and train transport officials  regarding the vulnerability of transport systems and infrastructure to climate change.

Measure.label: Early warning & emergency planning
Measure.definition: This parameter covers explicit mentions of an early warning system.

Measure.label: Transport Planning
Measure.definition: This parameter records any activities designed to raise the importance of resilience and adaptation in transport planning.

Measure.label: Relocation
Measure.definition: This parameter refers to efforts to relocate infrastructure or populations due to current or anticipated threats.

Measure.label: Redundancy
Measure.definition: This refers to the construction of redundant infrastructure/facilities, to prepare for the possible failure of existing systems.

Measure.label: Transport adaptation regulation
Measure.definition: This parameter identifies laws or regulations that focus on climate change adaptation in the transport sector.

Measure.label: Design Standards and updates
Measure.definition: This parameter refers to the adoption of  improved, more resilient design standards to effectively protect or reinforce transport facilities or infrastructure.

Measure.label: Other adaptation measures
Measure.definition: Other adaptation measures for transport not falling under the categories listed above.

Type of benefit.label: Air pollution reduction
Type of benefit.definition: Any policy content that makes a direct link between climate action on transport and air pollution as well as air quality improvements.

Type of benefit.label: Congestion reduction
Type of benefit.definition: Any policy references linking transport measures explicitly to improvements in traffic and less congestion.

Type of benefit.label: Better social inclusion
Type of benefit.definition: Policy content explicitly linking transport actions with social aspects, such as inclusion, equity or access to social services.

Type of benefit.label: Health Benefits
Type of benefit.definition: This parameter identifies health benefits that are attributable to transport measures in the document. It captures any policy content that establishes a direct connection between transport policy and human health.

Type of benefit.label: Improved accessibility
Type of benefit.definition: Transport measures that explicitly mention improved access or accessibility are captured here. The accessibility improvements can aim to promote jobs, education or essential services.

Type of benefit.label: Road safety improvements
Type of benefit.definition: The transport measure is captured by this parameter if it creates a direct linkage to road safety, accidents and road fatalities.

Type of benefit.label: SDG referenced in NDC transport measures
Type of benefit.definition: List all related SDGs in a single Cell, for example "SDG 1, SDG 3, SDG 9, SDG 11"

Type of benefit.label: Economic benefits
Type of benefit.definition: Reference to benefits for consumers or the national economy (e.g. positive impacts on the national trade balance through reduced imports of fossil fuels)

mode.description: Transport mode(s) a measure applies to (multi-select; columns flagged 'x' in the database).

geography.description: Spatial setting a measure applies to (multi-select).

activity.description: Whether a measure targets passenger or freight transport.

values.label: Not defined
values.definition: Measures where it it not clear whether the focus is on the transport of people or of goods

values.label: Passenger transport
values.definition: Measures targeting the transport of persons.

values.label: Freight
values.definition: Measures targeting the transport of goods

values.label: Both passenger and freight
values.definition: Measures targeting the transport of people and of goods.

status_of_measure.description: Implementation status of a mitigation measure.

asi.description: Avoid-Shift-Improve framework classification of a mitigation measure.
